questions.question: India formally joined which US-led initiative at the India AI Impact Summit 2026 to boost critical minerals and AI supply chains?
questions.explanation: At India AI Impact Summit 2026, India formally joined the Pax Silica coalition with the United States. Pax Silica is a strategic coalition of trusted nations to secure the full “silicon stack” from critical minerals and semiconductor fabrication to advanced Artificial Intelligence systems. It aims to reduce supply chain overdependence, prevent economic coercion and promote democratic control of emerging technologies. United States officials called it a roadmap for economic security and resilient technology partnerships.

questions.question: Which country announced its withdrawal from the Ottawa Convention (Mine Ban Treaty) in February 2026?
questions.explanation: Poland announced withdrawal from the Ottawa Convention to allow quick deployment of Anti-Personnel Landmines (APLs) along its eastern borders if needed. The Ottawa Convention, also called the Mine Ban Treaty, bans use, production, stockpiling and transfer of APLs. Member countries must destroy stockpiled APLs within 4 years and clear planted mines within 10 years. Extension of up to 10 years can be requested for mine destruction. Countries must submit annual reports on stockpiles, mine locations and destruction progress. The treaty came into force on 1 March 1999 and has unlimited duration.

questions.question: The Takeshima dispute, that was recently seen in news, is a long-standing territorial conflict between Japan and which country?
questions.explanation: South Korea protested Japan’s government-backed “Takeshima Day” (February 22), opposing Tokyo’s renewed sovereignty claim over the disputed islets. South Korea rejects calling it a dispute, while Japan continues diplomatic and symbolic assertions. The Takeshima dispute is a long-standing territorial conflict between Japan and South Korea (Republic of Korea). The islets, called Takeshima in Japan and Dokdo in South Korea, are located in the Sea of Japan, with South Korea currently controlling them and Japan claiming sovereignty.

questions.question: The GlobE Network was launched based on which initiative?
questions.explanation: India hosted the 12th Steering Committee Meeting of the GlobE Network. It was established in 2021 based on the Riyadh Initiative during G20 Saudi Arabia Presidency 2020. It is a global platform for anti-corruption law enforcement agencies. Its aim is to strengthen international cooperation to detect, investigate, and prosecute corruption cases. Membership is open to UN Member States and parties to United Nations Convention against Corruption. India is represented by Central Bureau of Investigation and Directorate of Enforcement.

questions.question: Balendra Shah has become youngest prime minister of which country?
questions.explanation: Balendra Shah became the Prime Minister of Nepal at the age of 35, making him the youngest leader in the country’s recent political history. He was sworn in by President Ram Chandra Poudel in Kathmandu, marking the end of a political transition triggered by Gen Z-led protests in 2025. The protests had overthrown the government of K. P. Sharma Oli and led to an interim administration headed by former Chief Justice Sushila Karki. Balendra Shah won the elections held in March 2026 as a candidate of the Rastriya Swatantra Party, which secured a near two-thirds majority, reflecting a strong public mandate for change.

questions.question: Punatsangchhu-II Hydroelectric Project, that was recently seen in news, is located in which country?
questions.explanation: Union Minister for Power and Housing and Urban Affairs Manohar Lal Khattar visited hydropower projects in Bhutan during his official visit (9–12 April). He inspected the Punatsangchhu-I Hydropower Project, where concrete pouring for the dam marked a key construction milestone. Punatsangchhu-II is a 1020 Megawatt run-of-the-river hydropower project. It is located on the right bank of the Punatsangchhu River in Wangdue Phodrang district of Bhutan. The Punatsangchhu projects are the largest India–Bhutan joint hydropower ventures, strengthening bilateral energy cooperation.

questions.question: Ismail Omar Guelleh has been re-elected as president of which country?
questions.explanation: Ismail Omar Guelleh was re-elected for a sixth term with 97.81% votes in Djibouti’s recent election. He has ruled Djibouti since 1999, succeeding his uncle Hassan Gouled Aptidon. His opponent Mohamed Farah Samatar was a former ruling party member; elections lacked strong competition. Opposition groups have often boycotted polls citing restrictions on political freedoms. Djibouti is strategically located near the Red Sea–Gulf of Aden route (major global shipping corridor). The country holds geopolitical importance for trade and military presence in the Horn of Africa region.

questions.question: In April 2026, which country has officially scrapped its post-World War II ban on lethal weapons exports?
questions.explanation: Japan has officially scrapped its post-World War II ban on lethal weapons exports, marking a major shift in its pacifist defense policy under Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi. The move allows exports of fighter jets, missiles, destroyers, and other defense equipment to 17 countries with signed defense equipment and technology transfer agreements, subject to approval by the National Security Council. The policy aims to strengthen Japan’s security amid regional threats from China, North Korea, and Russia, while also developing its domestic defense industry.

questions.question: The Taimoor missile system is developed by which country?
questions.explanation: Pakistan Navy successfully test-fired the indigenously developed Taimoor air-launched anti-ship cruise missile (ALCM). The missile was deployed jointly by the Navy and Pakistan Air Force, demonstrating multi-domain (air–sea) operational capability. The Taimoor missile system is developed by Pakistan. It is an indigenously developed, air-launched cruise missile (ALCM) designed for anti-ship and land-attack roles, capable of hitting targets up to 600 kilometers away. The system is meant to strengthen the Pakistan Navy and Air Force’s precision-strike capabilities.

questions.question: Lower Arun Hydropower Project is located in which country?
questions.explanation: Recently, Patel Engineering won a ₹1,593 crore contract for the Lower Arun Hydropower Project in Nepal. It is a 679 MW run-of-river project on the Arun River (a tributary of the Koshi River) in eastern Nepal. The project will be developed under the Build, Own, Operate, Transfer (BOOT) model. It is being developed by Investment Board Nepal and SJVN. It is a tailrace project of the Arun-3 Hydroelectric Project, meaning water released from Arun-3 is reused.
